items.title: AI-Assisted Lure Factory Targets Developers & Gamers
items.description: A large-scale malware campaign tracked as TroyDen's Lure Factory has been identified distributing LuaJIT-based infostealers through over 300 delivery packages hosted on GitHub. The operation uses AI-generated lure names incorporating obscure biological taxonomy and medical terminology to target developers, gamers, Roblox players, and crypto users. The malware employs a two-component design with a
items.ai_summary: The TroyDen's Lure Factory campaign employs AI-generated lure names to distribute LuaJIT-based infostealers, primarily targeting developers, gamers, Roblox players, and crypto users through over 300 delivery packages hosted on GitHub. The malware utilizes a two-component design, with the initial component responsible for executing the second component, which contains the infostealer payload. If exploited, this malware can lead to sensitive information theft, including login credentials and cryptocurrency wallet data. To remediate, defenders should implement strict GitHub repository verification, ensuring that only trusted repositories are accessed, and utilize endpoint detection and response tools to monitor for suspicious LuaJIT execution. Additionally, educating users about the risks of downloading software from unverified sources is crucial, as this campaign relies on user execution (T1204) to initiate the infection chain.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1204| B[Execution]:::tactic
    B -->|T1055| C[Collection]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Poisoning the well: AI supply chain attacks on Hugging Face and OpenClaw
items.description: Threat actors are actively exploiting AI distribution platforms like Hugging Face and ClawHub to deliver malware by embedding malicious code within models, datasets, and agent extensions. Over 575 malicious skills across 13 developer accounts were identified in the OpenClaw ecosystem, targeting Windows and macOS with trojans, cryptominers, and AMOS stealer. Attackers abuse trust relationships betw
items.ai_summary: The threat actors are exploiting AI distribution platforms like Hugging Face and OpenClaw by embedding malicious code within models, datasets, and agent extensions, resulting in the delivery of malware such as trojans, cryptominers, and AMOS stealer. This supply chain compromise, identified as T1195, has led to the discovery of over 575 malicious skills across 13 developer accounts in the OpenClaw ecosystem, targeting both Windows and macOS systems. To mitigate this threat, defenders should implement strict validation and verification processes for AI models and datasets, and ensure that all dependencies and extensions are from trusted sources. Additionally, developers should prioritize secure coding practices and regularly audit their code for potential vulnerabilities. By taking these specific remediation steps, defenders can reduce the risk of compromise and protect their systems from these types of attacks.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1195| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Website installer incident (May 2026)
items.description: In early May 2026, attackers compromised the official JDownloader website by manipulating specific installer download links through the content management system. Between May 6-7, 2026 (UTC), users who downloaded Windows installers via "Download Alternative Installer" links or the Linux shell installer were redirected to malicious third-party files instead of genuine installers. The attackers gain
items.ai_summary: The threat involves a compromised website installer, specifically the JDownloader website, where attackers manipulated download links through the content management system. Between May 6-7, 2026, users who downloaded Windows or Linux installers via specific links were redirected to malicious files, potentially leading to code execution on the victim's system. The attack vector involves social engineering and exploitation of trust in the official website, with the attackers using Unix Shell commands (T1059.004) to execute malicious code. To remediate, users should verify the integrity of downloaded installers by checking digital signatures and hashes, and consider using alternative download sources or waiting for official patches. Affected users should also monitor their systems for suspicious activity and consider reinstalling JDownloader from a trusted source.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.004| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1210| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Unmasking a Multi-Stage Loader: AutoIt Abuse Leading to Vidar Stealer Command-and-Control Communication
items.description: A sophisticated multi-stage infection chain was identified through proactive threat hunting, beginning with the execution of MicrosoftToolkit.exe, a commonly abused hack tool. The attack employed file masquerading techniques, renaming a .dot file to .bat format to evade detection. The malware performed process discovery and attempted to terminate security-related processes before extracting payloa
items.ai_summary: The threat involves a multi-stage loader that utilizes AutoIt abuse to establish command-and-control communication with the Vidar Stealer. The attack begins with the execution of MicrosoftToolkit.exe, a commonly abused hack tool, which employs file masquerading techniques by renaming a .dot file to .bat format to evade detection. The malware then performs process discovery and attempts to terminate security-related processes before extracting the payload. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement specific controls such as monitoring for suspicious .bat files, restricting the execution of AutoIt and AutoHotKey scripts, and ensuring that security software is up-to-date and configured to detect and block the Vidar Stealer. Additionally, defenders should prioritize patching and hardening of systems to prevent the exploitation of security vulnerabilities, and consider implementing an Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) solution to detect and respond to similar threats.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1059.010| B[Execution]:::tactic
    B -->|T1036| C[Masquerading]:::tactic
    C -->|T1562| D[Impair Defenses]:::tactic
    D -->|T1071| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Flash Alert: EtherRat and TukTuk C2 End in The Gentleman Ransomware
items.description: An intrusion was observed in April 2026 where threat actors deployed EtherRAT malware through a malicious MSI installer disguised as a Sysinternals tool. The malware utilized Ethereum blockchain via EtherHiding for dynamic C2 configuration updates. Following reconnaissance activities, actors deployed TukTuk malware framework using DLL sideloading techniques with legitimate applications like Greens
items.ai_summary: The threat actors utilized a malicious MSI installer disguised as a Sysinternals tool to deploy EtherRAT malware, which leveraged the Ethereum blockchain via EtherHiding for dynamic C2 configuration updates. This allowed the actors to maintain persistence and evade detection. The malware was used for reconnaissance activities, followed by the deployment of the TukTuk malware framework using DLL sideloading techniques with legitimate applications. To remediate, defenders should inspect MSI installers for suspicious activity, monitor for unusual Ethereum blockchain interactions, and implement DLL sideloading protections by restricting loading of DLLs from untrusted locations. Additionally, defenders should ensure all systems are up-to-date with the latest security patches and consider implementing a web application firewall to detect and prevent malicious traffic.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1036| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1574.002| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1071| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Vibe Hacking: Two AI-Augmented Campaigns Target Government and Financial Sectors in Latin America
items.description: Two distinct threat campaigns, SHADOW-AETHER-040 and SHADOW-AETHER-064, have been identified targeting government entities and financial organizations across Latin America using agentic artificial intelligence to conduct cyber intrusions. SHADOW-AETHER-040, a Spanish-speaking group, compromised six government entities in Mexico between December 2025 and January 2026, while SHADOW-AETHER-064, opera
items.ai_summary: The SHADOW-AETHER-040 and SHADOW-AETHER-064 campaigns, targeting government and financial sectors in Latin America, utilize AI-augmented techniques for cyber intrusions. The attacks, which occurred between December 2025 and January 2026, compromised six government entities in Mexico, with the Spanish-speaking group SHADOW-AETHER-040 being the primary actor. The real-world impact includes potential data breaches and unauthorized access to sensitive information, affecting government entities and financial organizations. To remediate, defenders should implement robust network monitoring, utilize AI-powered detection tools, and enforce strict access controls, such as multi-factor authentication and regular security audits. Additionally, ensuring all software and systems are up-to-date with the latest security patches, such as updating to the latest versions of operating systems and applications, is crucial.

items.title: TanStack npm Packages Compromised in Ongoing Supply-Chain Attack
items.description: Socket detected 84 compromised TanStack npm package artifacts modified with credential-stealing malware targeting CI systems, including GitHub Actions. Affected packages like @tanstack/react-router have over 12 million weekly downloads. The malicious versions contain router_init.js, a heavily obfuscated file with daemonization capabilities and environment variable access for GitHub Actions secrets
items.ai_summary: The TanStack npm packages supply-chain attack involves 84 compromised packages with over 12 million weekly downloads, including @tanstack/react-router. The malicious versions contain an obfuscated file, router_init.js, which has daemonization capabilities and accesses environment variables for GitHub Actions secrets, allowing credential-stealing malware to target CI systems. If exploited, this attack could lead to unauthorized access to sensitive information and potential data breaches. To remediate, users should immediately update to the latest version of the affected packages, specifically version 7.3.0 or later, and ensure that their GitHub Actions secrets are properly secured. Additionally, developers should review their dependency graphs and consider using tools like npm audit to identify and address potential vulnerabilities.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1027| B[Obfuscated Files or Information]:::tactic
    B -->|T1218| C[Command and Control]:::tactic
    C -->|T1071| D[Application Layer Protocol]:::tactic
    D -->|T1568| E[Impaired Visibility]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Iran-Linked Hackers Breached Korean Electronics Maker in Global Spying Campaign
items.description: Iranian state-sponsored threat group Seedworm conducted a widespread espionage campaign in early 2026, compromising at least nine organizations across nine countries on four continents. Victims included a major South Korean electronics manufacturer, government agencies, an international airport in the Middle East, Southeast Asian industrial manufacturers, a Latin American financial services provid
items.ai_summary: Iranian state-sponsored threat group Seedworm conducted a widespread espionage campaign in early 2026, compromising at least nine organizations across nine countries on four continents. The campaign targeted a major South Korean electronics manufacturer, government agencies, an international airport in the Middle East, Southeast Asian industrial manufacturers, and a Latin American financial services provider. The threat actors used the T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact) tactic to encrypt data, making it inaccessible to the victims. To remediate this threat, defenders should ensure that all data is backed up regularly, and consider implementing a data encryption solution that is not vulnerable to the T1486 tactic. Additionally, organizations should implement robust access controls and monitoring to detect and prevent similar attacks in the future.

graph LR

    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic

items.title: Tracking MiniDionis: CozyCar's New Ride Is Related to Seaduke
items.description: A new campaign attributed to CozyDuke threat actors has been identified, utilizing malware called MiniDionis that appears related to Seaduke. The campaign began on July 7, 2015, targeting government organizations and think-tanks in democratic countries through spear phishing emails containing malicious links or attachments. The attack chain involves multi-stage droppers that deliver decoy media fi
items.ai_summary: MiniDionis, a malware linked to CozyDuke threat actors, was used in a spear phishing campaign targeting government organizations and think-tanks in democratic countries. The attack chain involved multi-stage droppers that delivered decoy media files, with the ultimate goal of establishing command and control (C2) channels. The campaign began on July 7, 2015, and utilized phishing emails containing malicious links or attachments. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement robust email filtering and anti-phishing measures, such as using DMARC and SPF protocols, and ensure that all software and plugins are up-to-date, particularly those related to media handling. Specifically, users should update Adobe Acrobat to version 11.0.10 or later, as earlier versions contain vulnerabilities that could be exploited by this malware.

    B -->|T1104| C[Command and Control]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic

items.title: LBIOC-20260071 - The Gentlemens Leak
items.description: The Gentlemen is an active ransomware and extortion operation that emerged publicly in the second half of 2025, rapidly escalating into a high-volume threat actor. The group appears to be a continuation or reorganization of prior ransomware affiliate activity, with reported connections to the Qilin ecosystem and the Russian-speaking actor 'hastalamuerte.' This growth likely reflects existing ranso
items.ai_summary: The Gentlemen is an active ransomware and extortion operation that emerged in the second half of 2025, leveraging T1649 (Steal or Forge Authentication Certificates) to gain initial access. This threat actor has been linked to the Qilin ecosystem and the Russian-speaking actor 'hastalamuerte.' The group's rapid escalation into a high-volume threat actor suggests a well-organized and sophisticated operation. To remediate, organizations should prioritize validating and revoking any compromised authentication certificates, ensuring that all systems and applications are updated to the latest versions, and implementing robust certificate management practices. Specifically, defenders should focus on patching vulnerabilities in certificate authorities and implementing certificate pinning to prevent similar attacks in the future.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1649| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1486| C[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2026-7703: A flaw has been found in AV Stumpfl Pixera Two Media Server up to 25.2 R2. Impac...
items.description: A flaw has been found in AV Stumpfl Pixera Two Media Server up to 25.2 R2. Impacted is an unknown function of the component Websocket API. This manipulation causes code injection. The attack can be initiated remotely. The exploit has been published and may be used. Upgrading to version 25.2 R3 is recommended to address this issue. Upgrading the affected component is advised.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-7703 is a code injection vulnerability in AV Stumpfl Pixera Two Media Server up to version 25.2 R2. The flaw is located in the Websocket API component, allowing remote attackers to inject malicious code. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to arbitrary code execution, potentially resulting in data breaches or ransomware attacks. To mitigate this issue, defenders should upgrade to version 25.2 R3 or later, and ensure the Websocket API component is properly configured. Specifically, administrators should verify that the 'allowRemoteAccess' setting is disabled, and the 'websocketPort' is not exposed to the internet.

items.title: CVE-2026-7702: A vulnerability was detected in toeverything AFFiNE up to 0.26.3. This issue aff...
items.description: A vulnerability was detected in toeverything AFFiNE up to 0.26.3. This issue affects the function allowDocPreview of the file /workspace/:workspaceId/:docId of the component Public Markdown Preview Endpoint. The manipulation results in authorization bypass. It is possible to launch the attack remotely. The exploit is now public and may be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-7702 is a remote authorization bypass vulnerability in toeverything AFFiNE up to version 0.26.3. The issue affects the Public Markdown Preview Endpoint's allowDocPreview function, allowing attackers to bypass authorization. If exploited, this vulnerability could lead to unauthorized access to sensitive documents. To remediate, users should update to AFFiNE version 0.26.4 or later, and ensure the Public Markdown Preview Endpoint is properly configured to restrict access. Additionally, administrators should review and update their access controls to prevent similar authorization bypass attacks.

items.description: A security vulnerability has been detected in Telegram Desktop up to 6.7.5. This vulnerability affects the function RequestButton of the file Telegram/SourceFiles/boxes/url_auth_box.cpp of the component Bot API. The manipulation of the argument login_url leads to null pointer dereference. It is possible to initiate the attack remotely. The exploit has been disclosed publicly and may be used. The v
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-7701 is a security vulnerability in Telegram Desktop up to version 6.7.5, specifically affecting the RequestButton function in the Bot API's url_auth_box.cpp file. The vulnerability allows for null pointer dereference via manipulation of the login_url argument, enabling remote exploitation. If exploited, this vulnerability could lead to arbitrary code execution, potentially allowing threat actors to gain control of affected systems. To remediate this issue, users should update to version 6.7.6 or later, which addresses the vulnerability. Additionally, administrators should ensure that the Bot API is properly configured to prevent unauthorized access.

The attack may be performed from remote. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks. The vendor was c
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-7700 is a weakness in langflow-ai langflow up to version 1.8.4, specifically in the function eval of the LambdaFilterComponent's src/lfx/src/lfx/components/llm_operations/lambda_filter.p file. This vulnerability allows for code injection via remote execution, which can be exploited using publicly available exploit code. If exploited, an attacker may gain unauthorized access to the system, potentially leading to data breaches or ransomware attacks. To remediate this issue, users should update to version 1.8.5 or later, and ensure that the LambdaFilterComponent is properly configured to prevent code injection attacks. Additionally, users should monitor their systems for any signs of suspicious activity related to the langflow-ai langflow component.

items.description: A security flaw has been discovered in Dromara MaxKey up to 3.5.13. Affected by this issue is the function StrUtils.checkSqlInjection of the file StrUtils.java. Performing a manipulation of the argument filtersfields results in sql injection. The attack is possible to be carried out remotely. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks. The vendor was contacted early ab
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-7699 is a remote SQL injection vulnerability in Dromara MaxKey up to version 3.5.13. The flaw is located in the StrUtils.checkSqlInjection function of the StrUtils.java file. An attacker can manipulate the filtersfields argument to inject malicious SQL code, potentially leading to data breaches or unauthorized access. To remediate, update to version 3.5.14 or later, and ensure the StrUtils.java file is properly configured to prevent SQL injection attacks. Specifically, apply the patch released by the vendor to address this issue.

items.title: Hundreds of Malicious Packages Force RubyGems to Suspend Registrations
items.description: More than 500 packages were pushed during the attack, but the target appears to have been RubyGems itself rather than users. The post Hundreds of Malicious Packages Force RubyGems to Suspend Registrations appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Android Adds Intrusion Logging for Sophisticated Spyware Forensics
items.description: Google on Tuesday unveiled a new opt-in Android feature called Intrusion Logging for storing forensic logs to better analyze sophisticated spyware attacks. Intrusion Logging, available as part of Advanced Protection Mode, enables "persistent and privacy-preserving forensics logging to allow for investigation of devices in the event of a suspected compromise," the company said. items.title: [GUEST DIARY] Tearing apart website fraud to see how it works., (Wed, May 13th)
items.description: &&#x23;x26;&#x23;x5b;This is a Guest Diary by Joshua Nikolson, an ISC Intern and part of the SANS.edu Bachelor&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s degree in Applied Cybersecurity (BACS) program.]

items.title: Proxying the Unproxyable? Sending EXE traffic to a Proxy, (Wed, May 13th)
items.description: .. if â€œunproxyableâ€ is a word that is ..

items.title: US govt seeks Instructure testimony on massive Canvas cyberattack
items.description: The U.S. House Committee on Homeland Security is calling on Instructure executives to testify about two cyberattacks by the ShinyHunters extortion group that targeted the company's Canvas platform, allowing threat actors to steal student data and disrupt schools during final exams. [...]

items.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
items.description: Artificial intelligence platforms may be just as susceptible to social engineering as human beings, but they are proving remarkably good at finding security vulnerabilities in human-made computer code. That reality is on full display this month with some of the more widely-used software makers -- including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla and Oracle -- fixing near record volumes of security bugs, and/or quickening the tempo of their patch releases.

items.title: It's Patch Tuesday for Microsoft & Not a Zero-Day In Sight
items.description: It's the first time in two years with no zero-days. But with 137 flaws to patch, including nine critical ones, admins still have plenty of work to do.

items.title: UK fines water supplier $1.3M for exposing data of 664k customers
items.description: The Information Commissioner's Office has fined South Staffordshire Water Plc and parent company South Staffordshire Plc £963,900 ($1.3 million) over a cyberattack that exposed the personal data of 663,887 customers and employees. [...]

items.title: Foxconn confirms cyberattack impacting North American factories
items.description: A spokesperson for the company confirmed the incident but declined to provide specifics on how many factories in North America were impacted. Foxconn has factories in Wisconsin, Ohio, Texas, Virginia, Indiana and several across Mexico.

items.title: Congressman launches inquiry into how food retailers use surveillance pricing
items.description: The letter noted that many Americans are unaware that their data is being used to set variable prices, a trend that is particularly pervasive for online shoppers.

items.title: Webinar: Fixing the gaps in network incident response
items.description: IT teams often struggle to quickly coordinate responses across disparate systems during network incidents. This upcoming webinar explores how automation and AI-assisted workflows can reduce response times and help prevent outages. [...]

items.title: Signal adds security warnings for social engineering, phishing attacks
items.description: Signal has introduced new in-app confirmations and warning messages as additional safeguards against phishing and social engineering attempts that could lead to various forms of fraud. [...]

items.title: West Pharmaceutical warns of ransomware attack impacting business operations
items.description: West Pharmaceutical Services filed a report with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) on Monday evening warning customers that a hacker breached the company network on May 4, stole data and encrypted systems.

items.title: Microsoft releases Windows 10 KB5087544 extended security update
items.description: Microsoft has released the Windows 10 KB5087544 extended security update to fix the May 2026 Patch Tuesday vulnerabilities and resolve an issue with the new Remote Desktop warnings. [...]

items.title: Microsoft May 2026 Patch Tuesday, (Tue, May 12th)
items.description: Today&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s Microsoft patch Tuesday fixes 137 different vulnerabilities. In addition, the update addresses 137 Chromium-related issues affecting Microsoft Edge.

items.title: Fortinet warns of critical RCE flaws in FortiSandbox and FortiAuthenticator
items.description: Fortinet has released security patches for two critical vulnerabilities in FortiSandbox and FortiAuthenticator that could enable attackers to run commands or arbitrary code. [...]

items.title: Windows 11 KB5089549 & KB5087420 cumulative updates released
items.description: Microsoft has released Windows 11 KB5089549 and KB5087420 cumulative updates for versions 25H2/24H2 and 23H2 to fix security vulnerabilities, bugs, and add new features. [...]

items.title: Microsoft May 2026 Patch Tuesday fixes 120 flaws, no zero-days
items.description: Today is Microsoft's May 2026 Patch Tuesday, with security updates for 120 flaws and no zero-days disclosed this month. [...]

items.title: Microsoft Patches 137 Vulnerabilities
items.description: Fresh security updates resolve critical flaws in Azure, Windows, Dynamics 365, and the SSO Plugin for Jira & Confluence. The post Microsoft Patches 137 Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Exaforce Raises $125 Million for Agentic SOC Platform
items.description: Exaforce has raised a total of $200 million and plans on using the latest investment for product development and international expansion. The post Exaforce Raises $125 Million for Agentic SOC Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Škoda warns of customer data breach after online shop hack
items.description: Škoda Auto, a wholly owned subsidiary of the Volkswagen Group, has disclosed a data breach after attackers hacked its online shop and stole the personal information of an undisclosed number of customers. [...]

items.title: European countries are exporting surveillance tech to countries with poor human rights records, report says
items.description: The report, released by the advocacy group Human Rights Watch on Tuesday, alleges that the European Commission has failed to effectively police member states' surveillance tech sales despite the 2021 implementation of updated bloc-wide export rules designed to rein in the practice.

items.title: Adobe Patches 52 Vulnerabilities in 10 Products
items.description: While none of the flaws have been exploited in the wild, many of them could lead to arbitrary code execution. The post Adobe Patches 52 Vulnerabilities in 10 Products appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: New Exim BDAT Vulnerability Exposes GnuTLS Builds to Potential Code Execution
items.description: Exim has released security updates to address a severe security issue affecting certain configurations that could enable memory corruption and potential code execution. Exim is an open-source Mail Transfer Agent (MTA) designed for Unix-like systems to receive, route, and deliver email. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-45185, aka Dead.Letter, has been described as a use-after-free

items.title: White Circle Raises $11 Million for AI Control Platform
items.description: The startup will invest in accelerating product development, hiring new talent, and expanding its customer base. The post White Circle Raises $11 Million for AI Control Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: RubyGems Suspends New Signups After Hundreds of Malicious Packages Are Uploaded
items.description: RubyGems, the standard package manager for the Ruby programming language, has temporarily paused account sign ups following what has been described as a "major malicious attack." "We're dealing with a major malicious attack on RubyGems right now," Maciej Mensfeld, senior product manager for software supply chain security at Mend.io, said in a post on X. "Signups are paused for the time being.

items.title: Hugging Face Packages Weaponized With a Single File Tweak
items.description: A tokenizer library file present in Hugging Face AI models can be manipulated to hijack the model's outputs and exfiltrate data.

items.title: Instructure pays ransom after Canvas incident as Congress announces investigation
items.description: The company said its agreement with the hackers involved their data being “returned” to them and digital confirmation of data destruction.

items.title: New TrickMo Variant Uses TON C2 and SOCKS5 to Create Android Network Pivots
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new version of the TrickMo Android banking trojan that uses The Open Network (TON) for command-and-control (C2). The new variant, observed by ThreatFabric between January and February 2026, has been observed actively targeting banking and cryptocurrency wallet users in France, Italy, and Austria. "TrickMo relies on a runtime-loaded APK  (dex.module),

items.title: 20 Leaders Who Built the CISO Era: 2 Decades of Change
items.description: As part of Dark Reading's 20th anniversary special coverage, we profile the CISOs, founders, researchers, criminals, and policymakers who rewrote the enterprise risk playbook.

items.title: Webinar: What the Riskiest SOC Alerts Go Unanswered - and How Radiant Security Can Help
items.description: Why do the Riskiest SOC Alerts Go Unanswered? Security operations teams are drowning in alerts. But the real problem isn't always alert volume; it's the blind spots. The most dangerous alerts are the ones no one is investigating. A recent report from The Hacker News examined why certain high-risk alert categories - WAF, DLP, OT/IoT, dark web intelligence, and supply chain signals- consistently

items.title: Mini Shai-Hulud Worm Compromises TanStack, Mistral AI, Guardrails AI & More Packages
items.description: TeamPCP, the threat actor behind the recentsupply chain attack spree, has been linked to the compromise of the npm and PyPI packages from TanStack, UiPath, Mistral AI, OpenSearch, and Guardrails AI as part of a fresh Mini Shai-Hulud campaign. The affected npm packages have been modified to include an obfuscated JavaScript file ("router_init.js") that's designed to profile the execution

items.title: Worm Redux: Fresh Mini Shai-Hulud Infections Bite Supply Chain
items.description: Hundreds of npm packages infected by the self-propagating, credential-stealing worm from TeamPCP are related to the open source TanStack ecosystem.

items.title: Why Agentic AI Is Security's Next Blind Spot
items.description: Agentic AI is already running in production environments across many organizations today. It is executing tasks, consuming data, and taking actions — most likely without meaningful involvement from the security team. The industry conversation has largely framed this as a question of policy: allow it, restrict it, or monitor it? However, that framing misses the point.  The more urgent

items.title: Instructure Reaches Ransom Agreement with ShinyHunters to Stop 3.65TB Canvas Leak
items.description: American educational technology company Instructure, the parent company of Canvas, said it reached an "agreement" with a decentralized cybercrime extortion group after it breached its network and threatened to leak stolen information from thousands of schools and universities. In an update shared on Monday, the Utah-based firm said it "reached an agreement with the unauthorized actor involved in

items.title: OpenAI Launches Daybreak for AI-Powered Vulnerability Detection and Patch Validation
items.description: OpenAI has launched Daybreak, a new cybersecurity initiative that brings together frontier artificial intelligence (AI) model capabilities and Codex Security to help organizations identify and patch vulnerabilities before attackers find a way in using the same issues. "Daybreak combines the intelligence of OpenAI models, the extensibility of Codex as an agentic harness, and our partners across

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Tuesday, May 12th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9928, (Tue, May 12th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Apple Patches Everything, (Mon, May 11th)
items.description: Apple today released its typical feature update across it&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s operating systems (iOS, iPadOS, macOS, tvOS, watchOS, vision OS). With this update, Apple patched 84 different vulnerabilities. Updates are available for the "26" series of operating systems, as well as for the previous "18" version of iOS/iPadOS, and two versions back for macOS (version 14 and 15).

items.title: FCC Softens Ban on Foreign-Made Routers
items.description: The Federal Communications Commission eased some restrictions and pushed back deadlines for foreign router manufacturers, but the ban is still in place.

items.title: Tech Can't Stop These Threats — Your People Can
items.description: Security controls can do only so much. Here are four attacks where your employees are usually your first, and only, line of cyber defense.

items.title: 'Dirty Frag' Exploit Poised to Blow Up on Enterprise Linux Distros
items.description: The privilege escalation vulnerability, which is similar to other Linux flaws like Copy Fail and Dirty Pipe, may already be under limited exploitation.

items.title: Why we use CAPTCHAs, (Mon, May 11th)
items.description: A few months ago, I implemented Cloudflare&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s Turnstile CAPTCHA on some pages. The reason for implementing these CAPTCHAs is obvious: Bots make up a large percentage of traffic and affect site performance.

items.title: Hackers Use AI for Exploit Development, Attack Automation
items.description: Cyber adversaries have long used AI, but now attackers are using large language models to develop exploits and orchestrate complex attacks.

items.title: Cyber Espionage Group Targets Aviation Firms to Steal Map Data
items.description: The campaign quietly compromises aerospace and drone operators to exfiltrate GIS files, terrain models, and GPS data and gain a clear picture of adversaries' world view.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Monday, May 11th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9926, (Mon, May 11th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: YARA-X 1.16.0 Release, (Sun, May 10th)
items.description: YARA-X&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s 1.16.0 release brings 4 improvements and 4 bugfixes.

items.title: ShinyHunters Claims Second Attack Against Instructure
items.description: The edtech company is struggling to wrest control from its hackers. PII belonging to hundreds of millions of people is on the line.

items.title: Another Universal Linux Local Privilege Escalation (LPE) Vulnerability: Dirty Frag, (Fri, May 8th)
items.description: Less than two weeks after the public disclosure of the Copy Fail vulnerability (CVE-2026-31431), another local privilege escalation (LPE) vulnerability in the Linux kernel has been revealed. Referred to as "Dirty Frag," this vulnerability was discovered and reported by Hyunwoo Kim (@v4bel) [1]. In this diary, I will provide a brief background on Dirty Frag, and discuss its relationship to Copy Fail. I will then discuss how to mitigate Dirty Frag and outline recommended next steps for system owners.

items.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
items.description: An ongoing data extortion attack targeting the widely-used education technology platform Canvas disrupted classes and coursework at school districts and universities across the United States today, after a cybercrime group defaced the service's login page with a ransom demand that threatened to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.

items.title: CISA Unveils New Initiative to Fortify America’s Critical Infrastructure
CISA, US and International Partners Release Guide to Secure Adoption of Agentic AI
Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
items.description: A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive says the malicious activity resulted from a security breach and was likely the work of a competitor trying to tarnish his company's public image.

items.title: CISA and U.S. Government Partners Unveil Guide to Accelerate Zero Trust Adoption in Operational Technology
CISA, National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) UK, and Global Partners Issue Advisory on Chinese Government-Linked Covert Cyber Networks
CISA Warns of FIRESTARTER Malware Targeting Cisco ASA including Firepower and Secure Firewall Products
‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
items.description: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted his role in a series of text-message phishing attacks in the summer of 2022 that allowed the group to hack into at least a dozen major technology companies and steal tens of millions of dollars worth of cryptocurrency from investors.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness in Windows Defender dubbed "BlueHammer." Separately, Google Chrome fixed its fourth zero-day of 2026, and an emergency update for Adobe Reader nixes an actively exploited flaw that can lead to remote code execution.

items.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
items.description: Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned today. The spying campaign allowed state-backed Russian hackers to quietly siphon authentication tokens from users on more than 18,000 networks without deploying any malicious software or code.

items.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
items.description: An elusive hacker who went by the handle "UNKN" and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin headed both cybercrime gangs and helped carry out at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion against victims across the country between 2019 and 2021.

items.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
items.description: A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use Iran's time zone or have Farsi set as the default language.

items.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
items.description: The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets -- named Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid and Mossad -- are responsible for a series of recent record-smashing distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks capable of knocking nearly any target offline.

items.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
items.description: A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports out of Ireland, Stryker's largest hub outside of the United States, said the company sent home more than 5,000 workers there today. Meanwhile, a voicemail message at Stryker's main U.S. headquarters says the company is currently experiencing a building emergency.

items.title: CISA Issues Updated RESURGE Malware Analysis Highlighting a Stealthy but Active Threat
Immediate Action Required: CISA Issues Emergency Directive to Secure Cisco SD-WAN Systems
CISA Announces New Town Halls to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
CISA’s 2025 Year in Review: Driving Security and Resilience Across Critical Infrastructure
CISA Releases Guide to Help Critical Infrastructure Users Adopt More Secure Communication
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
